prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response:
Arrr, gather 'round ye scallywags and lend me yer ears, for Cap'n will tell ye the mystery of the great blue vault above!

Ye see, when the mighty sun - that blazin' doubloon in the heavens - fires its light down upon us, that light be carryin' all the colors of the rainbow within it, aye, every last one. But here be the trick, ye bilge rats - the sky itself, thin as it be, be full of tiny... particles, like the finest sea spray ye ever did sail through.

Now, the blue light, she be a feisty one - short and quick like a young deckhand - and she crashes and bounces off all them tiny bits of air every which way across the whole of the heavens. The red and the yellow, they be slow and long like an old sea dog, and they pass straight through without much quarrel.

So when ye look up from the deck of yer ship, the blue light be comin' at ye from EVERY direction across the whole sky, havin' bounced and scattered like a crew after a tavern brawl! And THAT, me hearties, be why the sky above looks as blue as the deep ocean waters we sail upon!

Now quit yer gawkin' at the heavens and get back to the riggin' before I have ye swabbin' the poop deck till Davy Jones comes callin'! ARRR!
